title: ferray-stride-tricks: sliding_window_view doesn't support axis parameter
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's sliding_window_view(x, window_shape, axis=None) supports an axis parameter to apply the sliding window along specific axes only. When axis is given, window_shape can be a scalar or a shorter tuple. ferray's sliding_window_view requires window_shape to match the array's ndim exactly. This means: (1) you can't do a 1D sliding window on a single axis of a 2D array without manual stride computation, (2) the common pattern sliding_window_view(arr, 3, axis=0) doesn't work. Add an optional axis: Option<&[usize]> parameter.
